steps.action: Open localhost:3200 and scroll the terminal demo into view
steps.expected: Terminal shell renders with macOS chrome, dark body, and monospace output
steps.observed: Terminal shell rendered with a titled header, three traffic-light dots at the top-left, dark shell background, and JetBrains Mono output

steps.action: Inspect terminal shell DOM and computed styles
steps.expected: Three dot elements, dark background, monospace font
steps.observed: `[data-terminal-dot="true"]` returned 3 dots; shell background was `oklab(0.129 -0.0038832 -0.0418201 / 0.9)`; panel font-family was `"JetBrains Mono", "JetBrains Mono Fallback"`

evidence.network: Initial document load observed via Performance API: GET http://localhost:3200/ (reload, 383ms, transferSize 21677); supporting CSS/fonts/scripts loaded from /_next/static/*

assertions.title: Tab bar displays all three demo commands

steps.action: Query all terminal tabs on initial load
steps.expected: Exactly three tabs with labels mktg init, mktg status, mktg doctor
steps.observed: Three tabs rendered with labels `mktg init`, `mktg status`, and `mktg doctor`

steps.action: Inspect the default active tab state
steps.expected: First tab active by default with distinct styling
steps.observed: `#terminal-demo-tab-init` was selected by default (`aria-selected="true"`) with emerald text/background/border styling distinct from the two inactive tabs

evidence.network: No tab-specific network activity; terminal demo is client-rendered from initial page assets

assertions.title: Typewriter animation types command then renders output

steps.action: Reload the page with the demo below the fold, then scroll the demo into view and observe over >4s
steps.expected: Command types character-by-character, pauses briefly, then output lines render progressively
steps.observed: Command text progressed from empty to `m`, `mktg`, `mktg i`, `mktg ini`, and `mktg init` over ~759ms, then output began appearing after a short pause and expanded line-by-line from 2 to 11 lines between ~1213ms and ~2423ms

steps.action: Capture before/after screenshots during the animation
steps.expected: Typing phase and output phase are visually distinct
steps.observed: `VAL-DEMO-003-typing-phase.png` shows the command mid-typing; `VAL-DEMO-003-output-phase.png` shows the rendered output phase

evidence.network: No network activity triggered by the animation after the initial page load

steps.action: Inspect the cursor element and observe visibility changes over time
steps.expected: Single cursor appears after the command, blinking roughly every second full cycle
steps.observed: Exactly one `[data-testid="terminal-cursor"]` element existed. Computed animation was `1.06s steps(2, start) infinite terminal-cursor-blink`, matching the expected 800-1200ms full cycle range

steps.action: Sample cursor visibility during runtime and capture paired screenshots ~550ms apart
steps.observed: Runtime samples showed cursor visibility toggling true/false across the same command position, and no duplicate cursor nodes were present; paired screenshots were captured 550ms apart

evidence.network: No cursor-specific network activity; blink is CSS animation only

assertions.title: Tab switching resets and replays animation

steps.action: Reload the page, scroll the terminal into view, then switch from the default init tab to the status tab mid-animation
steps.expected: Terminal body clears, active state changes, and the new command restarts from the beginning without leftover characters or lines
steps.observed: At 320ms the init command was mid-animation (`mktg init`, no output lines yet). After clicking `#terminal-demo-tab-status`, by 160ms the active tab had changed to `mktg status` and the command had restarted at `m` with no prior output remaining; by 860ms it had progressed to `mktg statu`, and by 1760ms the new demo output had started rendering

steps.expected: Status tab visibly active with replayed animation/output
steps.observed: `VAL-DEMO-005-tab-switch-status.png` shows the status tab active with its own replayed terminal content

evidence.network: No tab-switch network activity observed; demo content replayed client-side

steps.action: Allow the init demo output to finish and inspect rendered text styles
steps.expected: Green success indicators, yellow warnings, red errors, bold headers, and dim helper text with at least 3 distinct colors
steps.observed: Rendered output exposed 7 unique text colors. Green was used for `$` and `✓`, yellow for the warning line, red for the GitHub auth error line, brighter light text for main output, bold white for the heading, and dim slate text for the helper `Next step` line

evidence.network: No additional network requests; color styling came from preloaded CSS

steps.action: Inspect terminal panel overflow after the init demo finishes rendering
steps.expected: Terminal uses internal scroll with a fixed max-height and auto-scrolls to the bottom as output grows
steps.observed: `[data-testid="terminal-panel"]` had `overflow-y: auto` and `max-height: 384px`; after output completed, `scrollTop + clientHeight = 396`, matching `scrollHeight = 396` (bottom reached)

steps.action: Scroll the terminal panel back to the top
steps.expected: Earlier lines remain accessible inside the terminal viewport
steps.observed: After setting `scrollTop = 0`, the panel stayed at `clientHeight 384 / scrollHeight 396`, and the top of the command/output became visible again in the same terminal viewport

frictions.description: `agent-browser network requests` returned no captured entries for this static local page, so network evidence was gathered from the browser Performance API instead.
frictions.resolution: Recorded the navigation entry and first resource fetches via `performance.getEntriesByType()` and used that in the report.

summary: Tested 7 terminal demo assertions on http://localhost:3200 using browser session 0f916ecfbd72__u3. All 7 passed: the terminal chrome, tabs, typewriter replay, blinking cursor, tab reset/replay behavior, syntax-highlighted output, and internal scrolling all matched the validation contract.
